mechanism_narrative: Rising real sales (RSAFS) signal strengthening consumer demand and corporate revenue growth, which drives equity valuations higher. A sustained month-over-month increase in retail sales predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days through improved earnings expectations.
evidence_refs: fact:October 2017 retail sales at 486.5B, near cycle highs
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive RSAFS momentum fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate declines below 4.5% signal tight labor markets and wage pressure, which the market interprets as evidence of late-cycle expansion. This triggers equity repricing as investors anticipate Fed tightening, producing negative 20-day forward returns.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with Fed tightening bias
failure_condition: When UNRATE < 4.5%, S&P 500 produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC is negative and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate and inflation (CPIAUCSL) jointly determine real monetary conditions. When FEDFUNDS rises while CPIAUCSL growth slows, real rates tighten, compressing equity risk premiums and producing negative forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to produce negative 60d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 2% year-over-year, signaling robust supply-side capacity utilization and corporate profitability. This drives positive equity returns over 5 trading days as the market reprices earnings upward.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive INDPRO momentum fails to produce positive 5d returns in > 52% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly capture broad-based demand strength. When both rise simultaneously, it signals synchronized consumer and construction activity, predicting positive 20-day equity returns through multiplier effects on corporate earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:September 2017 HOUST at 1127K, October RSAFS at 486.5B
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces positive 20d returns in < 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates below 100K per month, signaling labor market softening and reduced earnings growth expectations. This triggers negative 60-day forward returns as equity valuations compress.
failure_condition: When 3-month PAYEMS growth < 100K, S&P 500 produces positive 60d returns in > 60% of instances, or Rank IC is positive and significant.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) and federal funds rate jointly determine monetary policy stance relative to economic slack. When GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annualized while FEDFUNDS remains below 2%, the Fed is accommodative, supporting equity valuations and producing positive 20-day returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 2.0% AND implied GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with solid growth
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces positive 20d returns in < 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while unemployment (UNRATE) rises simultaneously, signaling a supply shock or demand destruction. This dual deterioration predicts negative 5-day equity returns as the market reprices recession risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM < -0.5% AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp in 1 month
failure_condition: When both conditions trigger, S&P 500 produces negative 5d returns in < 55% of instances, or Rank IC is positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05).
